rules.name: allow-filesystem-read-operations
rules.description: Allows reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata anywhere on the filesystem.
rules.principle: Read-only exploration: The agent may only observe and query -- never modify, create, or delete. Broad read access: Reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata is permitted anywhere.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata anywhere.

rules.name: escalate-filesystem-write-create
rules.description: Escalates any file write, creation, or directory creation operation for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: Writing or creating files requires human approval per the constitution's no-mutations principle.

rules.name: escalate-filesystem-delete-move
rules.description: Escalates any file deletion or move operation for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: Moving files involves deletion of source and creation at destination, requiring human approval.

rules.name: allow-git-read-operations
rules.description: Allows reading git log, status, diff, branch info, and other read-only git information.
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to read git log, status, diff, and branch information.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits reading git log, status, diff, and branch information.

rules.name: escalate-git-mutation-operations
rules.description: Escalates all git operations that modify git state, history, or working directory for human approval.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase). No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: All git mutation operations require human approval per the constitution.

rules.name: allow-fetch-url
rules.description: Allows fetching any URL or performing web searches for reading content.
rules.principle: Network read access: Fetching URLs and querying remote APIs (GitHub, git remotes) is permitted for reading.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits fetching URLs and querying remote APIs for reading.

rules.name: allow-github-read-operations
rules.description: Allows read-only GitHub operations: listing/reading issues, PRs, branches, commits, releases, tags, repos, users, and code.
rules.principle: Network read access: The agent is allowed to list and read GitHub issues, pull requests, and repository metadata.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits listing and reading GitHub issues, pull requests, and repository metadata.

rules.name: escalate-github-write-operations
rules.description: Escalates all GitHub mutation operations (create, update, delete, merge, push, review write) for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval. The agent must ask for approval before any write, create, delete, or push operation.
rules.reason: GitHub write operations are mutations that require human approval per the constitution.
